narrative:
Temperatures finally warm a bit today as a warm front has finally passed to our north. But that puts us in a more unstable airmass that will render scattered afternoon and evening showers and thundershowers. Tuesday afternoon looks even more active for showers and thundershowers with slightly warmer temperatures. We'll call coverage "scattered" for Wednesday afternoon with a continuation of the warming trend.

The Memorial Day Monday Message is now available to subscribers. Drought relief is our top topic as the water faucet is back on!

Turning Warmer; Dodging PM Convection

Last Updated Mon May 25 at 11:15AM by Ray Russell

Temperatures finally warm a bit today as a warm front has finally passed to our north. But that puts us in a more unstable airmass that will render scattered afternoon and evening showers and thundershowers. Tuesday afternoon looks even more active for showers and thundershowers with slightly warmer temperatures. We'll call coverage "scattered" for Wednesday afternoon with a continuation of the warming trend.

The Memorial Day Monday Message is now available to subscribers. Drought relief is our top topic as the water faucet is back on!

Forecast Discussion

The road to warmer weather has been painfully slow on a holiday weekend. We make more progress towards warmer weather today.

A warm front is to our north, and a stalled front is to our west. We are in an increasingly unstable airmass. Expect scattered showers and thundershowers this afternoon and evening, and greater coverage Tuesday afternoon. Wednesday, it looks like we are back to scattered coverage.

A front pushes through from the north Thursday. We will have fewer showers and thundershowers Thursday followed by a somewhat cooler Thursday night.

That front stalls just to our south, and therein lies our problem for the rest of this forecast period. How far south is it, and where will a disturbance be Friday night and Saturday as it moves eastward along the front? I leaned the forecast to the drier side today but still mention the potential for showers, especially Friday night into Saturday.

By Sunday, the push of cooler air from the north should be enough to dry things out, but it will be a chilly end to May.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Monday Message for May 25, 2026

It's the Memorial Day Monday Message, where we recap our sorely-needed drought relief and discuss our region's cyclical warm/cool periods.

May 25, 2026
Rainy, Cool Saturday

It's a rainy, cool Saturday around western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia. High temperatures are running as much as 20-degrees below normal in the heart of the cold air damming zone.

Sunday and Memorial Day will continue to be unsettled, but temperatures will moderate and there will be additional breaks in the clouds, as the rainfall pattern becomes more convective, or showery in nature.

While the rainfall is falling on our holiday weekend, it's entirely beneficial with respect to our long-standing drought conditions.
